sensitivity: 0.076 average improvement across all readers (Site I: 0.097, Site II: 0.053)
specificity: -0.086 average change across all readers (Site I: -0.114, Site II: -0.06)
auc: 0.027 average improvement in AUC (Site I: 0.035, Site II: 0.018); standalone performance AUC: 0.73 (95% CI: 0.71-0.74)
intended_use: VisiRad XR is a computer-aided detection (CADe) device intended to identify and mark regions of interest that may be suspicious for lung nodules and masses on chest radiographs.
intended_use_full: VisiRad XR is a computer-aided detection (CADe) device intended to identify and mark regions of interest that may be suspicious for lung nodules and masses on chest radiographs.
target_condition: Lung nodules and masses on chest radiographs
ground_truth: Reference standard based on retrospective chest radiographs with expert evaluation; NLST (National Lung Screening Trial) dataset and data from two independent clinical sites (Level II trauma center in Montana and Level I trauma center in Colorado)
other_metrics: Standalone performance sensitivity: 0.83 (95% CI: 0.81-0.84) with average false positives per image of 1.5
